name: blood_bank_expiring_units_research_immunology
premise: Infection control wants a ward closed; bed management is refusing without a signed surge plan from the county. In Healthcare, the situation tagged internally around blood bank expiring units research immunology has converged with local pressure in the Stonehaven corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Finance notes that the fastest mitigation spends down a restricted reserve that another program legally depends on next quarter. A vendor’s root-cause paragraph contradicts your internal postmortem draft; both cannot be true, but neither is fully disprovable yet. Shift change is imminent; handoff cannot proceed without a signed disposition because liability follows the signatory line. No option cleanly satisfies both rights and utility; the least-wrong path requires naming who absorbs downside if the bet is wrong.

agents.name: Alex Kim
agents.identity: I am Alex Kim, the senior manager who must reconcile legal, ops, and customer-facing reality in one decision in Healthcare. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat utility as disposable collateral every time rights spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Ines Bennet
agents.identity: I am Ines Bennet, the risk and equity liaison pushing back on silent harms in Healthcare. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so rights cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes utility in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will freeze the riskiest workflow steps and staff a manual bridge until equity and error-rate checks clear—trading speed for defensibility on utility.
I will let the fastest operational path proceed under heightened monitoring and rollback triggers—trading some certainty on utility for immediate traction on rights.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Silence will be interpreted as a decision by everyone outside this room. Alex Kim must choose under rights versus utility. What does Alex Kim do?
